message:
fix(resolv): pure-Rust ns_datetosecs day calculation; tighten ns_sprintrrf SOA/SRV handling

Two libresolv parity fixes plus regression coverage.

ns_datetosecs (resolv_abi.rs)
  - Replace the host libc::timegm round-trip with a self-contained
    Gregorian → epoch calculator that:
      * validates year ∈ [1970, 9999], month ∈ [1, 12], hour ∈ [0, 23],
        minute ∈ [0, 59], second ∈ [0, 60], and day ∈ [1, days_in_month]
        with an explicit leap-year check, and
      * sums days/seconds with checked_mul/checked_add so any overflow
        past u32::MAX returns the libresolv error contract (errp = 1,
        result = 0) instead of silently wrapping.
  - Removes the libc::tm round-trip entirely, so the function no longer
    perturbs host TZ state or relies on timegm being available in the
    preload context (which it isn't, in a self-hosted libc).

ns_sprintrrf (resolv_abi.rs)
  - Drop the ad-hoc "6 -> SOA" / "33 -> SRV" mnemonic branches in the
    type-name writer. Those two records are formatted via the dedicated
    SOA / SRV rdata branches when the parser actually understands them;
    when format_rdata bails out for an unsupported / unknown encoding
    of those types, the result has to fall through to the generic
    "TYPE<n>" mnemonic + "\\# <len> <hex>" rendering — otherwise the
    output is a record whose mnemonic is "SOA" but whose rdata is the
    generic-encoding hex blob, which no resolver can parse.

resolv_abi_test.rs (regression coverage)
  - ns_datetosecs valid leap day "20240229000000" → 1709164800.
  - ns_datetosecs maximum representable u32 epoch
    "21060207062815" → u32::MAX; one second past → errp=1.
  - ns_datetosecs invalid non-leap day "20230229000000" → errp=1
    (proves we do not silently accept feb 29 in a non-leap year via a
    host libc that "normalizes" tm fields).
  - ns_sprintrrf NULL rdata pointer with non-zero rdlen returns -1.
  - ns_sprintrrf SOA (type 6) without recognised rdata renders as
    "TYPE6 \\# 3 010203" via the generic-encoding fallback rather than
    a mnemonic-with-hex hybrid.
  - ns_sprintrr handle bounds rejection: bogus _msg/_eom returns the
    failure contract instead of UB.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
